old_claim.note: Oral Tradition & Storytelling provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains because the corrected node covers archaeological abstract graphic signs rather than spoken narrative transmission.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract: reports an intentionally applied cross-hatched ochre drawing and says it demonstrates graphic-design ability; it does not claim oral storytelling as an enabling prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports visual graphic marking without requiring oral tradition or narrative transmission.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The old edge conflated visual-symbol evidence with spoken transmission; oral tradition can remain contextual for later myths or storytelling nodes.

ontology_before: Early Symbolic Communication depended directly on Oral Tradition & Storytelling through a generic enabling edge.
ontology_after: Early Symbolic Communication is scoped to source-backed visual marks; oral tradition remains a separate communication lineage rather than a direct prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: oral_tradition_storytelling is no longer a direct prerequisite for early_symbolic_communication.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to spoken symbolic communication rather than graphic signs.
A source showed the Blombos drawing or comparable early graphic signs depended on oral narrative transmission.
A downstream node required an oral-tradition path specifically through early_symbolic_communication.
short_reason: The source-backed scope is visual graphic signs, not oral storytelling.
